title: Company Bagh Summer Palace
description: The summer retreat palace built by Maharaja Ranjit Singh inside the Ram Bagh Gardens (commonly known as Company Bagh). It exhibits heritage Sikh architecture, beautiful woodwork, weapons, coins, and paintings from the reign of the Lion of Punjab.
address: Inside Ram Bagh Gardens, Mall Road, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: Constructed in 1818, it served as the royal summer escape for the King of Punjab, Maharaja Ranjit Singh.
Displays a rare collection of weaponry, including matchlock guns, swords, shields, and royal armor.
Exhibits old manuscripts, Sikh-era gold and silver coins, and a replication of the Koh-i-Noor diamond.
Surrounded by the historical Ram Bagh garden (Company Bagh) which features rare botanicals and old trees.

title: Maharaja Ranjit Singh Panorama
description: A unique multimedia museum in Ram Bagh that presents the life and battles of Maharaja Ranjit Singh in a grand circular format. Features a stunning 360-degree panoramic painting, battle sound effects, and detailed three-dimensional historical displays.
address: Ram Bagh Gardens, Mall Road, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: Features a massive, 12-meter-high, 360-degree cylindrical painting showcasing the six major battles fought by the Maharaja.
Synchronizes battle sounds, lights, and narrator explanations to make history feel like a movie.
Displays life-size figures of generals like Hari Singh Nalwa and the French and Italian commanders who trained the Sikh army.
Located in the middle of the beautiful Ram Bagh gardens, walking distance from the Summer Palace.

title: Partition Museum
description: The world's first museum dedicated to the 1947 partition of British India. Located in the grand Town Hall building, it documents the division of Punjab and Bengal through oral histories, letters, photographs, and personal memorabilia donated by survivors.
address: Town Hall, Katra Ahluwalia, Amritsar, Punjab 143006
bestTime: Mid-day hours (best to avoid weekend rush for reading the extensive displays).
bulletPoints: World's first physical museum dedicated to the partition of India and Pakistan, opened in 2017.
Houses 14 interactive galleries showcasing refugee artifacts like sewing machines, utensils, and trunk boxes carried across borders.
Features extensive newspaper clippings, boundary commission maps, and video logs of survivors telling their stories.
Concludes with the 'Gallery of Hope', where visitors write messages of peace and tie them on a barbed wire tree structure.

title: War Memorial & Museum
description: The Punjab State War Heroes' Memorial and Museum in Amritsar showcases the brave deeds and military history of Punjab from ancient times to the modern era. Features a massive 45-meter-high stainless steel sword monument, fighter jets, tanks, and galleries depicting battle history.
address: Amritsar-Attari GT Road Bypass, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bestTime: October to March (afternoon hours to enjoy the outdoor displays and indoor galleries).
bulletPoints: Features a stunning 45-meter-high stainless steel sword monument that stands tall at the entrance, representing victory.
Displays historical weapons, models of battlegrounds, war photos, and decommissioned fighter planes, tanks, and a model aircraft carrier.
Houses 8 state-of-the-art galleries detailing pre-British, British, and post-independence war achievements of Punjab soldiers.
Includes a 7D auditorium and a water laser show portraying the major battles fought by the Indian Army.

title: Digital Sikh Museum
description: Located underground in the Golden Temple Entrance Plaza, this museum uses high-tech digital projection, mapping displays, and documentaries to present the spiritual history of the Gurus and the Golden Temple itself.
address: Golden Temple Entrance Plaza, Golden Temple Complex, Amritsar, Punjab 143006
bestTime: Afternoon hours (great escape from the midday sun when visiting the Golden Temple).
bulletPoints: An underground, state-of-the-art multimedia gallery utilizing laser projections and immersive soundscapes.
Features 4 large galleries explaining the history of Sikhism, the compilation of Guru Granth Sahib, and the concept of Langar.
Uses projection mapping on physical models to show the historical evolution and reconstructions of the Golden Temple.
Provides audio-guided tours in multiple languages, making it popular with international tourists.
